correct_model.comment: Cost = the FULL always rule. The trailing never rule is INERT (first-match-wins: always already recorded uid=0). NO reduction.
correct_model.math: always execve ~1200 B. typical 50000*1200/1e9 = 0.06 GB/day -> $9.12/mo. The trailing never does NOT subtract anything (it never matches first for execve). Net = $9.12/mo typical, FULL band.

naive_wrong_model.comment: A naive impl that treats ANY never rule as suppressive regardless of position WRONGLY subtracts the uid=0 slice here.
naive_wrong_model.error: It would report net < $9.12/mo (subtracting a suppression that never happens, because the always rule matched uid=0 FIRST). The correct answer is the FULL $9.12/mo typical. Position (never AFTER always = INERT) is the load-bearing assertion; this is the exact INVERSE of scenario 9, where the same two rules in the opposite order DO suppress.
